business.headline: Gold rallies, oil prices retreat on optimism over US-Iran peace dealGold rose on Tuesday after an interim US-Iran deal eased pressure on oil prices. Lower inflation fears also reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike later this year.

Why India's wealthy are quietly buying second residencies abroadThey are not leaving India. But they want the option to, just in case. From Dubai to Portugal to Greece, promoters and business families are spending crores on second residencies abroad. It is not about emigration. It is about insurance.

8th Pay Commission: Memorandum deadline ends. What have unions asked forThe deadline for 8th Pay Commission proposals has ended, bringing employee and pensioner demands before the panel. The next phase will test which calls on salary, DA, fitment and pensions shape the final recommendations.

Sensex ends 544 points higher, Nifty nears 24,000; HUL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 544.15 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 76,808.48, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 135.25 points, or 0.57%, to close at 23,989.15, just shy of the crucial 24,000 mark.

11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longerThe US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

World's 1st trillionaire says a quadrillion-dollar fortune 'not impossible'Elon Musk said becoming the world's first quadrillionaire is "not impossible" and linked it to factories on the Moon and Mars. He also said dollars may no longer matter in a future shaped by AI, robots and interplanetary industry.

Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high as West Asia war fuels inflation fearsThe Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to 1% from 0.75%, the highest level since 1995, citing faster pass-through from crude oil prices. The move signalled concern that oil-led cost pressures could push inflation above target while keeping focus on gradual further hikes.

Dalal Street extends gains as Iran-US peace deal eases inflation concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 306.22 points, or 0.40%, to 76,570.55 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 72.95 points, or 0.31%, to 23,926.85 as of 9:36 am.

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions banning forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. The rules introduce refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents from January 1, 2027.

Govt hikes special duty on diesel, aviation fuel exports amid West Asia crisisThe Centre has increased the windfall profit tax on diesel exports to Rs 14 per litre and on ATF exports to Rs 12.5 per litre from June 16, while retaining the petrol export duty at Rs 1.5 per litre.

India-US trade deal enters final lap as fresh talks scheduled on June 23-24India and the United States will hold trade talks on June 23 and 24 to finalise the first tranche of their proposed bilateral trade agreement. The meeting could shape the pace of the wider pact as both sides work through market access and trade barriers.

The hidden intelligence gap in India's renewable energy transitionA utility-scale solar-plus-storage project in India was found to be losing Rs 20–25 lakh a month despite strong uptime. The case highlights why India's renewable build-out needs software-led grid and market optimisation.

Sensex ends 736 points higher, Nifty above 23,800; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex surged 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to settle at 76,264.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90. The rally was broad-based, with strong buying seen in auto, real estate, industrial and consumption-related stocks.

Why the US-Iran peace deal is good news for IndiaThe reported US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz has eased immediate pressure on India's oil-dependent economy. Its broader benefit will depend on whether lower crude prices and calmer trade routes hold.

Strait of Hormuz opens again, but will petrol and diesel prices fall?The Strait of Hormuz has reopened after a US-Iran understanding eased immediate supply concerns. The move may cool crude prices, but any relief in petrol, diesel and LPG is likely to be limited and gradual.

E20 fuel and car insurance: Will your claim be affected?ICICI Lombard has flagged that E20-linked damage claims in older vehicles may not be straightforward. The issue has sharpened concerns over whether fuel-related wear, negligence or policy exclusions could leave owners paying repair costs.

Why crude oil, monsoon and fertiliser prices are worrying the governmentNirmala Sitharaman said India faces risks from crude oil, foreign exchange, fertiliser prices and weaker rainfall. She said the government has kept buffer stocks ready as import and supply pressures persist.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points over US-Iran peace deal: Is worst over for investors?Markets surged after the US and Iran signalled a deal to end hostilities and restore Strait of Hormuz shipping. The rebound tracked falling crude prices, though analysts said investors should remain cautious.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points: Why is stock market rising today?The biggest trigger behind today's rally was a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The two countries indicated that an initial deal had been reached to end the conflict and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

entertainment.headline: Sanchita Ugale's actor-friend says she spoke of wanting to die, battled depression Actor Sanchita Ugale's friend and actor Geetanjali has urged people not to speculate on her death. She also claimed Ugale battled depression and insomnia.

Insulting women: Fresh FIR against comedian Pranit More over Rs 370 biryani row In fresh trouble for comedian Pranit More and techie Himanshu Jangra, the Gurugram Police filed a case over the 'Rs 370 biryani' remark, normalising sexual coercion and misogyny.

I stand by it: Dia Mirza doubles down on 'patriarchy causes climate change' claim Dia Mirza has explained her remarks linking patriarchy to climate change after online backlash. She said her comments were about unequal power structures driving extraction and environmental harm.

Actor Rainn Wilson says The Office can't be made today, calls it politically wrong Rainn Wilson has said The Office would be difficult to make today because of its politically incorrect humour. He said the show's clueless and offensive characters drove the comedy.

Mahadev and Sons faces backlash for romanticising domestic abuse in disturbing scene A scene in Mahadev and Sons showed Dheeraj slapping, pushing and hurting Rajji. The episode triggered outrage on social and renewed debate over portraying abuse on television.

Tom Holland confirms marriage to Zendaya, says family members were all there Tom Holland has confirmed he is married to Zendaya. The couple first sparked dating rumours during the filming of Spider-Man: Homecoming in 2016.

Chris Evans confirms he will return as Steve Rogers for 2027's Avengers: Secret Wars Chris Evans has confirmed his return for Avengers: Secret Wars after Avengers: Doomsday. The update extends Steve Rogers's MCU comeback and sharpens focus on Marvel's next two Avengers films.

Deepika Padukone's sister Anisha says actor's depression caught her off-guard Anisha Padukone said she did not recognise the signs when Deepika Padukone was dealing with depression. She said the experience pushed her to learn more about mental health and her own wellbeing.

Pakistani director Umar Nasir Ali reviews Main Vaapas Aaunga: Deeply emotional Pakistani filmmaker Umar Nasir Ali praised Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga. He called it a deeply emotional film that stays with you long after it ends. He also revealed that his own upcoming project is set against the backdrop of Partition.

Salman Khan seeks father Salim's permission before greeting fans, old video resurfaces An old video from the Sikandar trailer launch shows Salman Khan asking Salim Khan before stepping out to meet fans. The clip has resurfaced online as attention stays on Sikandar and his upcoming films.

Samantha confesses she's done films she's not proud of, explains why While promoting her upcoming Telugu film Maa Inti Bangaram, Samantha Ruth Prabhu reflected on her evolving relationship with cinema, admitting that she once chased stardom and took up films she wasn't proud of.

Filmmaker Raj Nidimoru refuses to say 'I love you' to Samantha on stage At the Maa Inti Bangaaram pre-release event, filmmaker Raj Nidimoru declined to say 'I love you' to his actor-wife Samantha despite the crowd's chants. He instead praised her work in the film, turning the moment into one of the event's main talking points.

Samantha admits she got addicted to stardom and became egoistic Samantha Ruth Prabhu said stardom drew her into a relentless cycle of work and expectations. She also reflected on how early success made her egoistic.

Bigg Boss 20: Jannat Zubair, Gullu, Ruru Thakur to enter Salman's show? Preparations for Bigg Boss 20 have picked up pace, with several television and digital personalities reportedly approached for the new season. The unconfirmed line-up points to a mix of familiar reality faces, social media stars and possible returning contestants.

Govinda wears a hair patch: Wife Ssunita asks, 'many heroes do it, why hide?' Ssunita Ahuja revealed that Govinda now uses a hair patch and said there is nothing unusual about it, as many actors rely on similar solutions.

Malayalam film Dridam hit by plagiarism row: Former IPS officer says story was hers IPS officer and writer Sreelekha R has accused the makers of Dridam of lifting the core idea of her short story Karinkudi Police Station. The claim has drawn fresh attention to the Shane Nigam film following its OTT release.

TV actor Sanchita Ugale dies by suicide at 26: What we know so far Television actor Sanchita Ugale died by suicide at her Nalasopara East home, and police have begun an investigation. Her family, friends and industry bodies have alleged mental pressure and sought a thorough probe.

PM Modi uses Allu Arjun film's song in Slovakia post, fans react Prime Minister Narendra Modi used the 23 Theme from Allu Arjun's upcoming film AA23 in a social media post from Slovakia. The post quickly drew fan reactions online and added to the film's growing buzz.

Why did female actors pass on Dil To Pagal Hai role? Karisma Kapoor reveals Karisma Kapoor recalled that many female actors did not want to play Nisha opposite Madhuri Dixit in Dil To Pagal Hai. She said the role broke heroine stereotypes and remains special for its emotional vulnerability.

general news.headline: Several Indians lost their lives: PM flags sailors' safety at G7 in Trump's presence Live updatesAddressing the Outreach Session at the G7 Summit in France, PM Modi said the conflict had inflicted significant human and economic costs on countries across the West Asia.

$500 million in value wiped out in a day: Turkey's Celebi on India ousterMore than a year after being forced out of Indian airports in the wake of Turkey's support to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor, Turkish aviation services firm Celebi Aviation says India's revocation of the security clearance granted to its Indian arm wiped out up to $500 million in value overnight.

PM Modi takes centre stage with Trump, Macron in front row of G7 leadersPrime Minister Narendra Modi's front-row position alongside US President Donald Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron in the official G7 leaders' photograph at Evian underscored India's growing global influence.

FRA 0-0 SEN Live: Mbappe off-colour as Senegal threatenFrance (FRE) vs Senegal (SEN) Live score, FIFA World Cup 2026: France are looking to find the right rhythm against Senegal, who have caused some early panic amongst the 2022 finalists. One of the big letdowns for France so far has been their captain Kylian Mbappe, who is yet to make a meaningful mark in this match.
Trump's Golden Hour is 24 Carat Humiliation for AmericaTrump wanted unconditional surrender from Iran, and he said so loudly. An unconditional surrender did happen. His.

We are most famous on Instagram: PM Modi, Meloni revive 'Melodi' craze at G7Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared another light-hearted exchange at the G7 summit that quickly caught the attention.

How Annamalai has switched to North-South politics, but with a differenceK Annamalai, after quitting the BJP, is seemingly rejigging the old political template of the North versus South debate in Tamil Nadu, with a new approach. Annamalai flagged the recent cases of crimes by "North Indian migrant workers" and sought that they be tackled through administrative interventions.

Pay or lose H-1B visa: Indian in US says Indian-origin CEO took $100K, files suitAn Indian H-1B worker, Rishikesh Raj Meesala, has accused his employer of forcing him to pay nearly $100,000 to keep his job and legal status in the US. Meesala, in his lawsuit, also alleged that threats of ICE were issued to him and his father was threatened after he resisted the demands made by his Indian-origin employer, Sai Jitender Kalagra.

Three satellites paint worrying monsoon picture with almost no rain cloud over IndiaFresh satellite images from global and Indian weather agencies show unusually sparse monsoon cloud cover over large parts of India. The pattern has raised concern over a prolonged rainfall deficit, though forecasts indicate a possible revival by late June.

Live: Successful partnerships are built on trust, PM Modi tells world leadersG7 Summit 2026 Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is attending the 52nd G7 Summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, at the invitation of French President Emmanuel Macron, joining leaders of the G7 nations and partner countries to discuss key global challenges including economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, artificial intelligence governance and sustainable development. Amid the summit's broader agenda, attention is focused on Modi's scheduled bilateral meeting with US President Donald Trump on Wednesday, where the two leaders are expected to discuss trade, visas, energy cooperation and wider strategic ties. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates from the G7 Summit and PM Modi's engagements on the sidelines of the global gathering.

Messi 200: Champions Argentina ready for landmark World Cup opener vs AlgeriaLionel Messi will walk out for his 200th match in the Argentina shirt when the defending champions begin their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against Algeria. With Argentina rallying around their captain and Algeria arriving as dangerous underdogs, the world champions begin their title defence in Kansas City.

SpiceJet staffer argues with flyers at Bengal airport in viral video, suspendedA verbal altercation broke out between SpiceJet staff and passengers at West Bagdogra Airport after flight to Delhi was cancelled.

Leave me: Video shows IAF officer's wife pleading in alleged conversion, rape caseAccording to the complaint, the woman's former classmate lured her to a hotel on the pretext of a property deal, drugged and raped her, recorded obscene videos, and later used them to blackmail and sexually exploit her. She also alleged being extorted of Rs 3.09 lakh and pressured to convert to Islam.

The naked Dancing Girl will stay ignored. Sorry, NCERT, we've failed youFor generations, Indian schoolchildren have turned not to an erotic but a blind eye to the 4,500-year-old Dancing Girl. By masking the Mohenjo-daro brass figurine's bare torso, the NCERT was trying to cultivate inquisitiveness in adolescents, who would have dug deeper. But we have failed the NCERT.

Uddhav Sena at crossroads as 6 of 9 MPs may defect before foundation dayThe possible defection could mirror the same pattern as the Trinamool Congress breakdown in West Bengal as the MPs might form a separate bloc and then join the Shinde Sena.

Elon Musk earns $165 billion in 1 day, nearly 2X of wealth held by Mukesh AmbaniElon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX raised a record $75 billion in its IPO. And the number, it seems, is only growing. To give you some context, Musk's single-day wealth gain was nearly double the entire net worth of India's richest man, Mukesh Ambani.

Insulting women: Fresh FIR against comedian Pranit More over Rs 370 biryani rowIn fresh trouble for comedian Pranit More and techie Himanshu Jangra, the Gurugram Police filed a case over the 'Rs 370 biryani' remark, normalising sexual coercion and misogyny.

Live: Iranian oil sale possible if Tehran says no to nukes, says US officialUS-Israel-Iran Live Updates: The United States and Iran took a major step towards ending their months-long conflict. US President Donald Trump announced that a memorandum of understanding between the two countries had already been signed ahead of a formal ceremony in Geneva on Friday. The framework agreement is expected to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and begin a 60-day negotiation process aimed at resolving outstanding disputes, including Iran's nuclear programme. Trump said shipping traffic had already started returning to the strait, helping ease concerns in global energy markets. However, the full text of the agreement remains unpublished, with further details expected in the coming days. Despite the diplomatic breakthrough, tensions persist across the region. Lebanon has emerged as a key point of contention, with Iran insisting the agreement requires an end to hostilities there, while Israel says it will continue acting against Hezbollah if necessary. Fresh Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon shortly after the deal was announced. In his first comments since the agreement became public, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reaffirmed that Israel would prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons regardless of any deal and said the country's security campaign was far from over. He also vowed that Israeli forces would remain in security zones where they see ongoing threats. To catch all the live updates, follow our live blog on indiatoday.in.

Leaks just moved to other apps: Telegram CEO on India banning app till NEET re-testThe comments come after Indian authorities temporarily blocked Telegram, citing its alleged use in attempts to defraud candidates appearing for a national medical entrance examination.

Telegram working despite ban, raises doubts on India's tech ability to block itIndia on Tuesday banned Telegram ahead of the NEET re-test on June 21. But hours after this ban, the app continues to largely work. It casts doubt on India's ability to block Telegram, which has some inbuilt tech safeguards against censorship and ban.

Monsoon slow, El Nino fast: Rain disappears from India as Pacific Ocean burnsIndia's southwest monsoon has slowed as El Nino intensifies in the Pacific and Nino 3.4 temperatures set repeated records. The contrast is raising concern over rainfall deficits during July and August, especially in rain-fed farm regions.

Rebellion, tussle and number game for Women's Quota and Delimitation Bill push?Ahead of the Monsoon Session, shifting alliances and rebel moves have reopened the numbers debate around the Women's Reservation and Delimitation Bills. The fresh arithmetic has triggered intense manoeuvring across parties as the government looks for the support or abstentions it still needs.

PM Modi, Trump shake hands at G7 Summit in France; first meeting in 16 monthsPrime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump shook hands at the G7 Summit in Evian, France, marking their first public meeting in 16 months.

ISI-backed terror module smuggling arms, drugs busted in Delhi; 7 arrestedDelhi Police has dismantled a Pakistan-backed terror-crime syndicate run by gangster-turned-terrorists Shahzad Bhatti and Ajmal Gujjar.

sports.headline: Neymar boost for Brazil: Superstar begins training ahead of Haiti World Cup clash Neymar has resumed individual training with Brazil after his right calf injury. The development lifts Brazil before the Haiti game, as they look to bounce back from their draw to Morocco.

Virat Kohli meets Jordan Cox in London before England Test debut Virat Kohli was seen with RCB team-mate Jordan Cox in London on the eve of the England batter's Test debut. The meeting drew attention as Cox prepares for a long-awaited chance at The Oval and Kohli's appearances in England continue to create buzz.

Snake trouble at World Cup 2026: Germany training base gets unexpected guest Germany captain Joshua Kimmich said a copperhead snake was spotted at the team's Winston-Salem training base. The incident has unsettled players and underlined an unusual off-field hazard for teams based across the United States.

FRA 0-0 SEN Live: Mbappe off-colour as Senegal threaten France (FRE) vs Senegal (SEN) Live score, FIFA World Cup 2026: France are looking to find the right rhythm against Senegal, who have caused some early panic amongst the 2022 finalists. One of the big letdowns for France so far has been their captain Kylian Mbappe, who is yet to make a meaningful mark in this match.

New Zealand in danger of early exit as Sri Lanka stun defending champions Women's T20 World Cup: Sri Lanka stunned defending champions New Zealand, winning by five wickets on Tuesday in Southampton. Nilakshika Silva's unbeaten half-century took Sri Lanka past the finish line.

Hashmaullah Shahidi urges Afghanistan to take ownership and make India grind IND vs AFG, 2nd ODI: Hashmatullah Shahidi said Afghanistan need to take greater responsibility and learn how to sustain pressure on India when the teams meet in the second ODI on Wednesday.

Reporters mock Son Heung-min's military service. South Korea players boycott press South Korea's players refused media interactions after leaked remarks mocking Son Heung-min's military status surfaced. The episode prompted a sharp KFA response and deepened tensions with sections of the domestic press.

Messi 200: Champions Argentina ready for landmark World Cup opener vs Algeria Lionel Messi will walk out for his 200th match in the Argentina shirt when the defending champions begin their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against Algeria. With Argentina rallying around their captain and Algeria arriving as dangerous underdogs, the world champions begin their title defence in Kansas City.

Richa Ghosh wary of Netherlands bowlers, India not willing to undermine debutants Women's T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs NED: Speaking to reporters ahead of Wednesday's match, Richa Ghosh said India would not take the Netherlands lightly despite them being tournament debutants, noting that they possess a strong bowling attack.

0.1 percent chance: Root digs old blazer from garage for Test captaincy return England vs New Zealand, 2nd Test: Joe Root never expected to captain England again, but a week of turmoil has forced the former skipper back into the role after dusting off a creased blazer from his garage and stepping into the spotlight once more.

Richa Ghosh credits Harmanpreet, Smriti for helping her overcome lean patchRicha Ghosh credited Smriti Mandhana, Harmanpreet Kaur, and head coach Amol Muzumdar for backing her through a lean patch, saying their advice helped her stay positive and trust the process.

Action against Sooryavanshi? BCCI, Team India play down Dambulla controversyWhile BCCI Secretary Devajit Saikia has ruled out disciplinary action against Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for his post-match outburst in Dambulla, senior India coach Sairaj Bahutule has hit back at the Sri Lanka A players for provoking the 15-year-old prodigy.

Pakistan penalised for slow over rate after humiliating defeat vs IndiaPakistan's disappointing Women's T20 World Cup campaign suffered another setback after the ICC fined the team five per cent of their match fee for a slow over-rate during their 64-run defeat to India at Edgbaston.

Blow for Thomas Tuchel's England as Tino Livramento gamble backfiresFIFA World Cup 2026: England defender Tino Livramento has reportedly been ruled out before their opener after a hamstring injury. England begin their campaign on Wednesday in a blockbuster Group L game against Croatia.

Ex-India batter pushes for Yastika Bhatia in XI despite crushing win vs PakistanWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs NED: Former India batter Hemlata Kala suggested one change to the Indian playing XI, saying that Yastika Bhatia should be included given her recent good form.

Shree Charani inches closer to No.1 T20I ranking after heroic spell vs PakistanWomen's T20 World Cup: Shree Charani returned figures of 4-0-21-3 against Pakistan in Birmingham on Sunday, moving within touching distance of becoming the No. 1-ranked bowler in Women's T20Is.

Grandmaster Anish Giri checkmates football Twitter after day of drawsWhen the FIFA World Cup's opening day ended in four draws, chess grandmaster Anish Giri logged on. What followed was a masterclass in accidental rage-baiting.

Maja Chwalinska rewarded with Wimbledon wildcard after dream French OpenWimbledon 2026: After her dream run at the French Open, Poland's Maja Chwalinska has been rewarded with a wildcard for Wimbledon. The 24-year-old recently enjoyed a stunning rise in the WTA rankings, climbing from World No. 114 to a career-high No. 21.

Another Indian-origin footballer impresses on FIFA World Cup debutNew Zealand's attacking midfielder Sarpreet Singh became the first Indian-origin player to start in the FIFA World Cup. Sarpreet impressed on his debut as New Zealand played out a thrilling 2-2 draw against Iran.

Serena Williams makes Grand Slam comeback, to play doubles with sister VenusWimbledon 2026: Serena Williams has made her Grand Slam comeback and will play doubles with sister Venus Williams. Serena last played majors back in 2022 when she advanced to the third round of the US Open.

Football without Mexico has no meaning: Ochoa eyes retirement after FIFA World CupGuillermo Ochoa has suggested the 2026 FIFA World Cup could be the final chapter of his playing career, saying he no longer sees a reason to continue in football once his time with the Mexico national team comes to an end.

Smriti Mandhana only Indian in Time's list of Most Influential SportspersonsIndia vice-captain Smriti Mandhana has been named in TIME magazine's list of the 100 Most Influential People in Sports for 2026. She is the only Indian in the list led by LeBron James.

Iran says team asked to leave US immediately after drawing 2-2 with New ZealandIran coach Amir Ghalenoei criticised FIFA's treatment of his team, calling them the tournament's "most oppressed" side after they were instructed by US authorities to leave the United States and return to their base in Tijuana immediately following the draw with New Zealand.

Iran supporters turn up in numbers with banned 'Lion and Sun' flagIran's World Cup opener against New Zealand in Los Angeles unfolded before a crowd filled with Lion and Sun flags. The scenes underscored how identity and dissent shaped the night as much as the 2-2 draw.

Tunisia appoint Herve Renard as new manager after sacking Sabri LamouchiTunisia have sacked coach Sabri Lamouchi following a woeful start to their World Cup campaign and have reached an agreement to replace him with fellow Frenchman Herve Renard, Tunis Afrique Presse reported on Tuesday. Tunisia became the first team to change coaches at the tournament after suffering a heavy opening defeat.

India to host Asia Cup of fencing between June 19 and 24 in New DelhiIndia will host the Asian Senior Fencing Championships for the first time in New Delhi from June 19 to 24. The selection of the Asian Games squad will be based on this tournament.

VAR official clarifies viral white supremacist gesture, says it was a twitchFIFA World Cup: VAR referee Shaun Evans was called out on the internet for making a white supremacist gesture on live broadcast. Evans has issued an official clarification after the social media outrage.

India women's hockey team beat Japan 2-1 to secure semifinal berthIndia's 2-1 victory over Japan in Auckland was a significant result in the 2025-26 FIH Women's Nations Cup. The win gave India six points from two matches, ensured a semi-final berth, and kept them at the top of Pool A.

Meet Merlin, the Mexico City duck that became the World Cup's viral mascotMerlin, a two-year-old duck from Mexico City, has become one of the most unexpected stars of the FIFA World Cup after videos of him celebrating Mexico's opening win over South Africa went viral online.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
